title: taraxacum vacillans
intro: Taraxacum vacillans, also known as the creeping dandelion, is a type of flowering plant in the dandelion family. It is a perennial herb with medicinal properties, particularly in its roots and leaves, which contain sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and other bioactive compounds. The plant has been used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, skin conditions, and respiratory issues due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. Taraxacum vacillans can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, and it is also used in traditional medicine as a diuretic and to support liver health.
